speaker: interviewer
text: Today is September 4th, 1996. The survivor is Lili Meier. And this tape five. [SIGHS] Do you believe in God?

speaker: interviewee
text: Oh, yes. I-- I feel him here, tapping my shoulders.

speaker: interviewer
text: What does God say to you? [SIGHS]

speaker: interviewee
text: That as long as I had my parents, they must have been very proud of you. And I feel that whatever I did afterwards, I done it for myself, but also for my parents. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] I had a good life. Of course, health wise, I-- I suffer. There are days that I don't-- I can't go on because both are artificial. But as long as I see the world, nobody is-- the way they-- they would be. And I'm still grateful to him, for watching over me, my family.

speaker: interviewer
text: Do you often think about the Holocaust? [SIGHS]

speaker: interviewee
text: If I think, in the-- in the end, sometimes, I come on with a little anger. And that hurts me. But I-- why should I complain? I have so much to live for. Wonderful children, wonderful grandsons, a good husband. My first love was good. Eric is a good husband. And--

speaker: interviewer
text: Why did you decide to make this tape and to tell your story now?

speaker: interviewee
text: Because time passes on. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] And we all live in the world. Maybe-- and-- and-- and this time, of the game. And it's something that we all-- all the survivors have a duty to do that. Not so much for us, ourselves, but for our future generations.

speaker: interviewer
text: When you look back upon your incredible experiences, why do you suppose that you survived?

speaker: interviewee
text: I guess go back to [INAUDIBLE]. Knowing that's in a safe place, well taken care of, it was a big responsibility to-- to me. Because we don't have an alarm system in Miami. Here, we have it. And we have to share it with the world while we are still here.

speaker: interviewer
text: What's your most painful memory of the Holocaust? [SIGHS]

speaker: interviewee
text: That I was robbed of something. I'm not the only one. But I'm speaking for myself and for the rest of the survivors. So the day has come that we have to leave-- leave this legacy to our future generations.

speaker: interviewer
text: Mhm.

speaker: interviewee
text: They should know of it. And as much as Germany's trying to-- to shrug it off from their-- from their shoulders, we shouldn't think of them. We should think of the future.

speaker: interviewer
text: Before we end, do you have any message that you would like to leave us with? [SIGHS]

speaker: interviewee
text: I would like to thank you, Eric, very much. Also, the survivors are very much in debt. Debt to Steven Spielberg. Because he-- he has done something that nobody else did. And that will always be there, here.

speaker: interviewer
text: I would like to thank you, to wish you a life of Shalom.

speaker: interviewer
text: All right.

speaker: interviewer
text: Would you please introduce this very good-looking gentleman who is sitting next to you?

speaker: interviewee
text: That's my husband, Eric.

speaker: interviewer
text: Mr. Meier? ERIC (HUSBAND): Hmm? Would you like to tell us something about your wife? ERIC (HUSBAND): Well, I think she's a wonderful person, very persistent. And she knows what she's doing. Is there anything that you would like to add, that you might have forgotten?

speaker: interviewee
text: Not right now.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. Again, thank you very, very much.

speaker: interviewee
text: Don't thank me.

speaker: interviewer
text: Would you please tell me who's in this picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: My uncle.

speaker: interviewer
text: And his name?

speaker: interviewer
text: [? Leo ?] what?

speaker: interviewee
text: Weis.

speaker: interviewer
text: And next to him?

speaker: interviewee
text: Is my aunt.

speaker: interviewer
text: And her name?

speaker: interviewee
text: [? Rivka. ?]

speaker: interviewer
text: And next to your aunt?

speaker: interviewee
text: Is my mother, Esther.

speaker: interviewer
text: Do you know when this was taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: Around 1936.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where was this taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: In Bilky.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you. Would you please tell me who's in this picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: The first one is me. I was 10 years old.

speaker: interviewer
text: And then?

speaker: interviewee
text: And then my three brothers came after me.

speaker: interviewer
text: And their names?

speaker: interviewee
text: Moshe, Zrilu-- no, I'm sorry. Moshe. Zisl, [? Hershel. ?]

speaker: interviewer
text: And when was this taken? Do you know?

speaker: interviewee
text: This was taken around '36. I was 10 years old there.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where was this taken? [SIGHS]

speaker: interviewee
text: In our house.

speaker: interviewer
text: In Bilky?

speaker: interviewee
text: Bilky.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you. Would you please tell me what's in this picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: This picture is the--

speaker: interviewer
text: It's the top one.

speaker: interviewee
text: The top was where the hospital was. And about 600 feet is where the crematorium was.

speaker: interviewer
text: The hospital, located where?

speaker: interviewee
text: In Nordhausen-Dora.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK.

speaker: interviewer
text: Would you please tell me what's in this picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: The hospital in Nordhausen-Dora is on top. And then to the left on the bottom are the crematoriums from Nordhausen-Dora.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you. Would you please tell me what this is a picture of?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is a picture-- [PAUSES FOR 6 SECONDS] --of the original album-- [PAUSES FOR 6 SECONDS] --that they made for the book cover.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you very much. Would you-- can you tell me who's in this picture, please?

speaker: interviewee
text: Those are my two youngest brothers, in front of the crematorium.

speaker: interviewer
text: And their names?

speaker: interviewee
text: In Birkenau, in Auschwitz. Short Zrilu and Selig.

speaker: interviewer
text: And when was this taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: It was taken in Auschwitz.

speaker: interviewer
text: When?

speaker: interviewee
text: 1944, upon our arrival.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you. Would you please tell me about this picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: That is a picture of arrival in Birkenau. And we are here, photographed, in front of the kitchen.

speaker: interviewer
text: And the year?

speaker: interviewee
text: 19-- [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] May, 1944.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you.

speaker: interviewer
text: I'm going to go ahead and pull in this picture and then we'll also do a zoom. Also do it tighter.

speaker: interviewer
text: Sounds fine. What I'm going to ask you to do is point at you. I'm going to say-- or you could just say, this is me in the picture. Or--

speaker: interviewee
text: OK.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. So but you-- you will need to point.

speaker: interviewee
text: Do I need the glasses or just go blindfolded?

speaker: interviewer
text: Can you find yourself?

speaker: interviewee
text: Not without the glasses.

speaker: interviewer
text: Oh, OK. Well, then we'll stop.

speaker: interviewee
text: I'm giving you a hard time. I don't mean to.

speaker: interviewer
text: No, not at all.

speaker: interviewee
text: Right here.

speaker: interviewer
text: Well, that's-- that's good.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, we'll do a tighter one too. [POINTS]

speaker: interviewee
text: OK, go ahead. I take my hands away?

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. So when Lili does-- do you want Lili to point? Or just say, I'm next to the lady in the flower dress?

speaker: interviewer
text: No, she can point, I think.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK.

speaker: interviewer
text: Point. [POINTS] OK. And pull your finger out.

speaker: interviewer
text: So when do you want her to point?

speaker: interviewer
text: I just did it.

speaker: interviewer
text: Oh, we're done?

speaker: interviewer
text: Yeah. [LAUGHTER]

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. [BACKGROUND NOISE]

speaker: interviewer
text: Whoa. Did you turn your mic off?

speaker: interviewer
text: Would you please tell me who's in this photo?

speaker: interviewee
text: My husband, my ex, who unfortunately passed away 20 years ago. And he's holding me up in the-- to the skies.

speaker: interviewer
text: Where was this taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: It was taken in Miami, when we arrived from Czechoslovakia.

speaker: interviewer
text: And do you recall what year this was taken?

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you.

speaker: interviewer
text: No, I'm saying hold on for a second.

speaker: interviewer
text: Oh.

speaker: interviewer
text: But I just-- they-- they said they wanted--

speaker: interviewer
text: OK.

speaker: interviewer
text: The reflection [INAUDIBLE].

speaker: interviewer
text: Could you please tell me about this photograph?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes. We went to turn-- Eric and I went to turn over the book to Yad Vashem in 1980. And then we were taken to the Knesset.

speaker: interviewer
text: And who was with you in this picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: We loved Prime Minister Begin. He was a great person.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you. [PAUSES FOR 20 SECONDS] Would you please tell me who's in this picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: My husband, Eric, and me.

speaker: interviewer
text: And when was this taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: Well, 17 years ago.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you. Would you please tell me who's in this photo?

speaker: interviewee
text: My-- our daughter, Esther, and Fred.

speaker: interviewer
text: And when was this taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: It was taken at Scotty's bar mitzvah.

speaker: interviewer
text: And when was that?

speaker: interviewee
text: I forgot the year.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, thank you.

speaker: interviewee
text: '80? No. Something like 1980.

speaker: interviewer
text: It's OK. [PAUSES FOR 7 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: Speed.

speaker: interviewer
text: Hmm?

speaker: interviewer
text: Go ahead.

speaker: interviewer
text: Would you please tell me who's in this picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: Again, Esther, Fred, Scott, and Gary.

speaker: interviewer
text: And when was this taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: At Scott's bar mitzvah.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you. [PAUSES FOR 15 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: Go ahead.

speaker: interviewer
text: Would you please tell me who's in this photograph?

speaker: interviewee
text: That's my grandson, Gary. He is recently in-- has been three years in Tel Aviv, going to medical school.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you. [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS] Would you please tell me who's in this photo?

speaker: interviewee
text: Scott, my oldest grandson.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] Would you please tell me who's in this picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: In this picture is our two grandsons of ours. Michael, to the right, and Adam, to the left.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] Would you please tell me who's in this picture? No no, don't point.

speaker: interviewee
text: Our son-in-law, Peter. Next to him is daughter, Bonnie. Next to Bonnie is Michael. And the end is Adam.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you very much.
